all: Audit-remediation work must read ARCHITECTURE_OPPORTUNITIES.md, BUILD_SYSTEM_OPPORTUNITIES.md, EFFECT_TS_OPPORTUNITIES.md, and FP_IMMUTABILITY_OPPORTUNITIES.md before choosing fixes.
Do not edit generated artifacts: packages/**/build/**, packages/**/dist/**, generated *.d.ts, *.d.ts.map, *.js.map, editor backups, or packages/domain/src/schemas/*.js.
Use pnpm/corepack when available. Do not run build/codegen commands that write build/ or dist/ artifacts unless a stage explicitly approves it.
Run API and tmux gates through bot scripts on localhost:3100 so user-owned localhost:3000 servers can keep running; keep network gates serial.
For Flag Hack feature work, use the project default graph flag-hack-feature-gated unless the user explicitly asks for the audit graph or a one-off custom graph.
Use PI_NPX_OK=1 npx -y pnpm@9.10.0 for pnpm commands in this repository.
Feature verification must include a real tmux run of the CLI that exercises the requested behavior, not just unit tests or static review.

flag-hack-audit-gated.description: Flag Hack audit remediation workflow with generated-file guardrails and unit, performance, Effect API, and tmux CLI E2E gates.

stages.title: Plan audit finding remediation
stages.description: Read the four audit docs, choose bounded findings, name touched source files, and identify required gates before editing.
promptInstructions: Read ARCHITECTURE_OPPORTUNITIES.md, BUILD_SYSTEM_OPPORTUNITIES.md, EFFECT_TS_OPPORTUNITIES.md, and FP_IMMUTABILITY_OPPORTUNITIES.md.
Plan only source/config/docs/test changes. Do not edit packages/**/build/**, packages/**/dist/**, generated declarations/maps, editor backups, or packages/domain/src/schemas/*.js.
Name the expected gates: pnpm generated:guard, pnpm check, pnpm test:unit, pnpm test:perf, pnpm test:api:bot, and pnpm test:e2e:tmux:bot.
stages.purpose: Plan bounded remediation for selected audit findings before editing.
acceptanceChecks: Generated-file policy is part of the plan
isolationBoundary: Do not edit implementation files during planning
Do not broaden beyond the selected audit findings

stages.title: Implement bounded audit remediation
stages.description: Make the smallest source/config/docs/test change for the planned audit finding while preserving generated-file guardrails.
promptInstructions: Use TDD for observable behavior changes. Add or update focused tests first when a harness exists.
Do not edit packages/**/build/**, packages/**/dist/**, *.d.ts, *.d.ts.map, *.js.map, editor backups, or packages/domain/src/schemas/*.js.
Use pnpm/corepack if available. If install or lockfile update cannot run, make source/config changes and report the blocker exactly.
stages.purpose: Implement the selected audit remediation within the approved write scope.
acceptanceChecks: Gate commands are added or updated when needed
isolationBoundary: Do not commit or push
Do not run build/codegen that writes build/dist artifacts

stages.title: Run generated guardrails and typecheck
stages.description: Run pnpm generated:guard and pnpm check, then classify failures as code, pre-existing, or environmental.
promptInstructions: Run pnpm generated:guard && pnpm check.
Do not run codegen/build commands that write packages/**/build/** or packages/**/dist/**.
If pnpm is unavailable, run node --check for guard scripts and report the exact blocker.
stages.purpose: Verify generated-file guardrails and TypeScript health.
acceptanceChecks: pnpm check is attempted or blocked with exact reason
isolationBoundary: Do not edit files during verification

stages.title: Run unit gate
stages.description: Run pnpm test:unit for package smoke/unit coverage.
promptInstructions: Run pnpm test:unit and report exact test files or failures.
Do not edit generated artifacts while diagnosing failures.
stages.purpose: Verify unit smoke coverage for audit remediation.
isolationBoundary: Do not edit files during verification

stages.title: Run performance smoke gate
stages.description: Run pnpm test:perf and capture benchmark smoke output.
promptInstructions: Run pnpm test:perf. Treat it as a smoke benchmark until baselines are explicitly added.
Do not edit generated artifacts while diagnosing failures.
stages.purpose: Verify benchmark smoke coverage exists and runs.
isolationBoundary: Do not edit files during verification

stages.title: Run Effect API smoke gate
stages.description: Run pnpm test:api:bot against a disposable bot-port server and validate getWorld/getLogs/getInventory/noop action.
promptInstructions: Run pnpm test:api:bot. It owns localhost:3100 for the duration of the gate and must clean up the server child process.
If port 3100 is already occupied, report it as an environmental blocker rather than using the existing process; do not kill a user-owned server on port 3000.
stages.purpose: Verify the Effect HttpApi smoke runner against a disposable server.
isolationBoundary: Do not reuse unrelated local servers
Do not edit files during verification

stages.title: Run tmux CLI E2E smoke gate
stages.description: Run pnpm test:e2e:tmux:bot, capture terminal output, and clean up the tmux session.
promptInstructions: Run pnpm test:e2e:tmux:bot. It requires tmux and owns localhost:3100 while running.
Report the terminal capture path and whether the unique tmux session was cleaned up.
If tmux is unavailable, run tmux -V when possible and report the exact blocker.
stages.purpose: Verify terminal CLI smoke behavior through tmux automation.
outputs: tmux E2E evidence and capture path
acceptanceChecks: Terminal capture path is reported when tmux runs
isolationBoundary: Clean up tmux sessions on success and failure
Do not edit files during verification

stages.title: Review audit remediation and gate evidence
stages.description: Review the diff, generated-file guardrails, four audit-doc alignment, and unit/perf/API/tmux evidence.
promptInstructions: Review changed files against ARCHITECTURE_OPPORTUNITIES.md, BUILD_SYSTEM_OPPORTUNITIES.md, EFFECT_TS_OPPORTUNITIES.md, and FP_IMMUTABILITY_OPPORTUNITIES.md.
Confirm generated artifacts were not edited and pnpm generated:guard evidence is present.
Confirm unit, performance, API, and tmux E2E gate evidence is present or blockers are exact. Do not commit or push.
stages.purpose: Review the audit remediation diff and validation evidence.
outputs: Prioritized review findings or PASS report
acceptanceChecks: All four requested gate types are reviewed
isolationBoundary: Do not edit files during review
Do not commit or push

flag-hack-feature-gated.description: Flag Hack default feature workflow with planning, implementation, typecheck, unit, disposable server restart, lint, tmux smoke E2E, feature-specific tmux use verification, code review, and an explicitly approved commit stage.

stages.title: Plan Flag Hack feature/change
stages.description: Create an implementation-ready plan for the requested Flag Hack change, including touched files, focused tests, tmux feature verification strategy, and gate commands.
promptInstructions: Inspect current git status and preserve unrelated dirty/untracked files, especially chatgpt.txt.
Plan source/config/test changes only; do not edit generated/build/dist/declaration/map/schema JS artifacts.
Identify focused unit tests and a real tmux feature verification scenario using pnpm test:feature:tmux:bot or equivalent bot-port tmux automation.
Name required gates: generated:guard, check, test:unit, format:check, lint, test:api:bot, test:e2e:tmux:bot, and test:feature:tmux:bot.
stages.purpose: Plan a bounded Flag Hack feature/change before editing.
outputs: Validation and tmux feature verification plan
acceptanceChecks: Plan names touched files and tests
Plan includes feature-specific tmux use verification
isolationBoundary: Do not edit implementation files during planning
Do not commit or push

stages.title: Implement Flag Hack feature/change
stages.description: Make the planned source/config/test changes with focused tests and generated-file guardrails.
promptInstructions: Use TDD for behavior changes. Add or update focused tests before or alongside implementation.
Do not edit packages/**/build/**, packages/**/dist/**, *.d.ts, *.d.ts.map, *.js.map, editor backups, or packages/domain/src/schemas/*.js.
Preserve unrelated dirty/untracked files and do not stage chatgpt.txt.
If a requested verification stage lacks tooling, add minimal project tooling under scripts/ and package.json to make it possible.
stages.purpose: Implement the planned Flag Hack change within the approved source/test/tooling scope.
isolationBoundary: Do not commit or push
Do not run build/codegen that writes build/dist artifacts unless explicitly approved

stages.title: Run generated guard and TypeScript check
stages.description: Run generated-file guardrails and root TypeScript project check.
promptInstructions: Run PI_NPX_OK=1 npx -y pnpm@9.10.0 generated:guard.
Run PI_NPX_OK=1 npx -y pnpm@9.10.0 check.
Do not edit generated artifacts while diagnosing failures.
stages.purpose: Verify generated-file guardrails and TypeScript health.
isolationBoundary: Do not edit files during verification

stages.title: Run focused and full unit tests
stages.description: Run focused tests for touched behavior and the root unit suite.
promptInstructions: Run focused tests for changed files/behavior first when applicable.
Run PI_NPX_OK=1 npx -y pnpm@9.10.0 test:unit.
Report exact failures and test files.
stages.purpose: Verify unit coverage for the change.
outputs: Focused and full unit test evidence
isolationBoundary: Do not edit files during verification

stages.title: Restart disposable Flag Hack server
stages.description: Verify the Flag Hack server can start cleanly on the bot port from the current workspace and then shut it down to leave user port 3000 free.
promptInstructions: Run PI_NPX_OK=1 npx -y pnpm@9.10.0 test:api:bot as the disposable server restart/API smoke gate.
This gate owns localhost:3100 only for its duration, must leave no server running, and must not disturb user-owned localhost:3000.
If port 3100 is already occupied, report an environmental blocker rather than killing unrelated processes; do not kill a user-owned server on port 3000.
stages.purpose: Verify a fresh Flag Hack server process can start, serve API requests, and stop cleanly.
acceptanceChecks: Server cleanup reported or environmental blocker recorded
isolationBoundary: Do not reuse or kill unrelated local servers
Do not edit files during verification

stages.title: Run format and lint gates
stages.description: Run repository formatting check and lint after compile/unit/server smoke.
promptInstructions: Run PI_NPX_OK=1 npx -y pnpm@9.10.0 format:check.
Run PI_NPX_OK=1 npx -y pnpm@9.10.0 lint.
If formatting fails only for touched files, format only touched files and rerun.
writeScope: validation report only, or touched-file formatting if needed
isolationBoundary: Do not edit unrelated files during verification

stages.title: Run tmux CLI smoke E2E
stages.description: Run the repository tmux E2E smoke script against a disposable server and CLI.
promptInstructions: Run PI_NPX_OK=1 npx -y pnpm@9.10.0 test:e2e:tmux:bot.
This gate owns localhost:3100 while running and must clean up tmux sessions/server children without disturbing localhost:3000.
Report terminal capture paths and exact blockers.
stages.purpose: Verify baseline terminal CLI behavior through tmux automation.
outputs: tmux smoke E2E evidence and capture paths
acceptanceChecks: Terminal capture path reported when tmux runs
isolationBoundary: Clean up tmux sessions on success and failure
Do not edit files during verification

stages.title: Exercise the requested feature in tmux
stages.description: Run the actual CLI in tmux and exercise the requested feature with observable terminal assertions, extending the feature harness when needed.
promptInstructions: Design a feature-specific terminal scenario for the user request.
Prefer PI_NPX_OK=1 npx -y pnpm@9.10.0 test:feature:tmux:bot with FLAGHACK_TMUX_KEYS as a JSON array and FLAGHACK_TMUX_EXPECT/FLAGHACK_TMUX_REJECT regexes.
If the requested feature needs richer assertions than scripts/tmux-feature-check.ts supports, add minimal tooling before marking this stage passed.
This stage must run the project in tmux and exercise the feature itself. Unit tests or code review alone are not sufficient.
Report tmux session/pane identifiers when available and the terminal capture path.
stages.purpose: Verify the specific requested feature in a real terminal session.
outputs: Feature-specific tmux evidence, keys/scenario, and capture path
acceptanceChecks: Terminal output/assertions demonstrate the requested behavior or exact blocker
writeScope: scripts/** if harness extension is necessary
isolationBoundary: Clean up project-created tmux sessions
Do not kill unrelated processes
Do not edit product code during verification unless rerouting to implementation

stages.title: Code review diff and gate evidence
stages.description: Review the implementation, tests, generated-file hygiene, server restart evidence, lint, e2e, and feature-specific tmux evidence.
promptInstructions: Review the diff for bugs, type-safety, generated-file hygiene, and project conventions.
Confirm compile, unit, bot-port server restart/API smoke, format/lint, bot-port tmux E2E, and feature-specific bot tmux evidence is present or blockers are exact.
Do not commit or push.
stages.purpose: Review the Flag Hack change and validation evidence before handoff.
outputs: Prioritized review findings or PASS report
acceptanceChecks: All requested verification stages are reviewed
isolationBoundary: Do not edit files during review
Do not commit or push

stages.title: Commit approved Flag Hack feature/change
stages.description: Create a git commit for the completed, reviewed, and validated change when commit approval is explicitly granted.
promptInstructions: This stage is mechanically gated by task graph commit approval and must remain skipped unless commit approval is explicitly granted for the run.
Before committing, inspect git status and ensure unrelated files such as chatgpt.txt are not staged.
Commit only the reviewed implementation and validation-supporting changes from this task graph.
Use a concise conventional commit message that matches the completed change.
Do not push; pushing requires a separate explicit approval/stage.
stages.purpose: Commit the completed Flag Hack feature/change after validation and code review when explicitly approved.
outputs: Git commit hash or explicit skipped/blocked report
acceptanceChecks: Commit approval is present before staging or committing
Commit hash is recorded when the stage runs
writeScope: git index and commit metadata for reviewed task changes only
isolationBoundary: Do not modify source files during commit stage
Do not stage chatgpt.txt or unrelated local files
Do not push
